description_long: This object is a tall, vertical metal structure featuring multiple cylindrical components and attached electrical equipment. It has several large, round signal or insulator elements mounted at different heights, with a lattice-like crossarm extending horizontally from the main pole. There are boxy enclosures and a circular dish or shield element attached to the side, likely for electrical or communication purposes. The surface appears weathered and industrial, with a dark, metallic finish and some signs of wear. The structure is robust and designed to support heavy electrical components, with visible mounting brackets and support arms. Its overall appearance suggests it is intended for outdoor installation in urban or roadside environments.
description: A tall, dark metal tower with cylindrical insulators, a lattice crossarm, and attached electrical boxes and a circular shield. The structure is industrial and weathered, designed for supporting electrical equipment.
description_view_0: A narrow, head-on view showing the vertical alignment of the pole and the stacked cylindrical components.
description_view_1: A side view revealing the circular shield, boxy enclosures, and the lattice crossarm extending outward.
description_view_2: A rear view highlighting the cylindrical insulators and the depth of the attached components.
description_view_3: An angled view showing both the circular shield and the arrangement of the crossarm and insulators.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: There is a single horizontal crossarm mounted at mid-height, with a lattice structure extending perpendicular to the main pole.
attribute_configuration_of_attached_powe: Power lines are attached in a parallel configuration, likely supporting a single circuit with three-phase arrangement, routed horizontally from the crossarm.
